title: Eye detector: side profiles and non-face cores
oneline: All five night false-flags cleared, every true fail retained, fully offline
summary: Instrumentation showed the two false-flag mechanisms: wheel hubs pass the STRONG_LIGHT_SIDE reference bar because they are legitimately light-on-dark by day, and duck-wide's band is 74% solid pen-pupil ink so the detector cannot see its dark pupil. Two suppressions in judgeNightEyes fix both: skip band-blind cores (annulus ink fraction > 0.5) and, on chalk-forked pages, skip cores with no chalk-white nearby in the night composite (the committed chalk is effectively the human-blessed eye annotation idea (a) asked for). Full-catalog audit: 58 -> 53 flagged, all 5 false-flags cleared, caterpillar/ladybug/snail true fails retained, light column byte-identical.
tried: Full-catalog baseline audit (94 pages) tabulating night false-flags vs true fails
Per-core instrumentation of light/night luma, band extremes, and annulus pen-ink fraction
Fix (c'): chalk-white-near-core suppression on chalked pages (CHALK_WHITE_MIN=245)
Fix for duck: band-blind core skip via annulusInkFrac > 0.5
Idea (a) sketch: generated draft eye-annotations JSON for all 94 pages
Idea (b) side-profile count relaxation: analyzed and rejected
worked: Chalk-white suppression clears monster-wide (7), rover-wide, fire-tall, train-wide; the chalk doubles as the committed per-page eye annotation
Band-blind skip clears duck-wide (annulus 74% solid pen ink; true fails sit at 0.26-0.29, threshold 0.5 separates cleanly)
True fails retained: caterpillar-wide 2, ladybug-wide 2, snail-wide 1; light column (teddy/police fails) byte-identical
failed: Idea (b) single-eye relaxation rejected: duck's issue is measurement blindness, not eye count; relaxation would weaken true-fail retention
snail-wide (human-judged fine per pipeline.md) still fires: its stats are indistinguishable from caterpillar's true fail with the current band
limitations: CHALK_WHITE_MIN=245 assumes compositeNight renders chalk ink at ~255; recalibrate if the composite gains blur
BAND_BLIND_INK_FRAC=0.5 calibrated on 14 pages (gap 0.36 vs 0.54); a band-blind genuinely-dead eye would be skipped
Generator path (gen-coloring-fills-dark) uses the same judge but was verified by reading only (no paid generation)
Chalk-white suppression only applies to chalked pages; un-forked pages keep the old (noisy) behavior by design
